description:
## Goal

Extend the refinement eligibility predicate beyond ABI shift.
Recover the per-arg-value optimizations the canonical-first
pivot conservatively gave up.

Two new triggers:

1. **Constant fold**: under the callsite's narrowing, the typer
   proves the body's return-Var has a literal Descr. Refinement
   body collapses to a trivial 'return literal' instruction.

2. **Dead clause**: the narrowing kills ≥1 pattern row in a
   multi-clause fn. Refinement body has fewer branches; smaller
   CLIF, faster path.

## What lands

- Extend Eligibility enum (already declared in fz-rfn.2):
  ```
  enum Eligibility {
      AbiShift,
      ConstFold,        // newly active
      DeadClause,       // newly active
      UserSpec,         // still future
  }
  ```

- Eligibility predicate now runs a SHADOW typing pass on the
  body under the candidate narrowing:
  - Type the body using the narrowed key as entry Descrs (no
    body emission, just typing).
  - Inspect the result:
    a) If every Return statement's Var has a literal Descr →
       eligibility includes ConstFold.
    b) If the pattern matrix's `find_unreachable_rows()` under
       the narrowing kills ≥1 row that's live under canonical →
       eligibility includes DeadClause.
  - Multiple triggers can fire for the same refinement; record
    all.

- Emission policy unchanged from fz-rfn.3: AotO2 emits all
  eligible refinements regardless of which trigger fired.

- Codegen for ConstFold refinement: trivial body, just
  `return iconst(lit)` or equivalent. Much smaller than
  canonical's body.

- Codegen for DeadClause refinement: lower body under narrowing,
  pattern matrix skips killed rows, fewer branches emitted.

## What gets removed

- The conservatism of 'AbiShift only' from fz-rfn.2's v1.
- The fz-aiz.7 conservatism that all narrow specs needed bodies —
  ConstFold refinements collapse to literal returns; DeadClause
  refinements emit only live branches.

## Expected effect

- fib(0, 0, 1): ConstFold refinement emits a trivial body that
  returns 1. Caller's direct call resolves to it. (Today this
  is one of the few cases that still works in the existing
  narrow-spec model; we recover it under the new model.)
- ast_eval_env's eval({:num, lit}, _): ConstFold fires if the
  narrowing pins both the tag and the int literal. Refinement
  returns the literal directly.
- Multi-clause fns where one call site only matches one clause:
  DeadClause fires; refinement body is the single-clause version.

## Acceptance

- fib_tailrec's expected.clif.aot includes ConstFold refinements
  for [0, 0, 1] and [1, 0, 1] (the literal-result narrowings).
- Multi-clause fixtures (multi_clause, classify_two_clause) show
  DeadClause refinements where appropriate.
- All matrix tests still pass.
- spec-quality diagnostic reports the eligibility trigger
  alongside refinement records.

## See also

- fz-rfn.3 — emission policy (blocking).
- src/pattern_matrix.rs — find_unreachable_rows for DeadClause
  detection.
title: fz-rfn.5: constant-fold + dead-clause eligibility extensions
